stem: A racecar is traveling at 176 feet per second on a closed circuit. What is the car's speed, in miles per hour? (Use 1 mile = 5,280 feet)
explanation:
To convert feet per second to miles per hour, two independent unit conversions must be applied simultaneously — one for distance (feet to miles) and one for time (seconds to hours). This is a compound rate conversion requiring the student to track both unit dimensions at once.

Step 1 — Identify the required conversion chain:
$$\frac{176 \text{ ft}}{1 \text{ s}} \times \frac{1 \text{ mi}}{5{,}280 \text{ ft}} \times \frac{3{,}600 \text{ s}}{1 \text{ hr}}$$

Step 2 — Cancel units:
$$= \frac{176 \times 3{,}600}{5{,}280} \text{ mi/hr}$$

Step 3 — Calculate:
$$= \frac{633{,}600}{5{,}280} = 120 \text{ mi/hr}$$

The car's speed is $\boxed{120}$ miles per hour.

distractorRationale.A: Correct answer. Applies both conversions properly: multiplies by 3,600 s/hr and divides by 5,280 ft/mi.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong conversion factor error: uses 1 mile = 5,000 feet instead of 5,280 feet. Calculation: $176 \times 3{,}600 \div 5{,}000 = 126.7$ mph. This represents a plausible rounding error for a student who misremembers or estimates the conversion factor.
distractorRationale.C: Inverted distance conversion error: multiplies by 5,280 instead of dividing (treats the factor as feet per mile in the wrong direction). Calculation: $176 \times 5{,}280 \div 3{,}600 = 258.1$ mph. The student correctly inverts the time conversion but inverts the distance conversion in the wrong direction.
distractorRationale.D: Inverted time conversion error: divides by 3,600 and multiplies by 5,280 in the wrong arrangement, effectively using 1 mile = 1,760 feet (yards per mile). Calculation: $176 \times 3{,}600 \div 1{,}760 = 360.0$ mph. This arises from a student confusing 1,760 yards/mile with 5,280 feet/mile and then inverting the time factor.
